title: Sweet cornbread
subtitle: Northern American-style, cakey and moist
excerpt: A soft, sweet American-style cornbread baked in a square tin. Buttermilk keeps the crumb tender; the fine cornmeal gives mild sweetness and golden colour. Done in 25 minutes.
sourceNotes: American cornbread divides along regional lines: Southern cornbread is unsweetened, cast-iron baked, and made with white cornmeal; Northern cornbread (associated with New England and the Midwest) uses yellow cornmeal, sugar, and a higher flour ratio for a cakey texture. This recipe follows the Northern tradition documented in Joy of Cooking (Rombauer, Becker and Becker, 2006 edition) and Maida Heatter's Book of Great American Desserts (1985).

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Wrap squares individually and freeze for up to 2 months. Thaw at room temperature or warm in a low oven.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cornbread is best fresh and warm. It dries out quickly; store in an airtight tin and use within 2 days.

content.text: Cornbread is mixed in under 5 minutes. The rule is the same as all quick breads: stir the wet into the dry until just combined, then stop. Lumps are fine. A smooth batter is a sign of overmixing, which will tighten the crumb.
If you do not have buttermilk, stir 1 tablespoon of lemon juice into 240 ml of whole milk and leave for 5 minutes. The acid curdles the milk slightly and gives the same tenderising effect.

Heat the oven to 190°C fan. Grease and line a 20 x 20 cm square tin. Whisk together the {{cornmeal}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} in a large bowl. In a jug, beat the {{eggs}} with the {{buttermilk}} and melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and fold together until just combined. The batter should be thick and pourable.

Pour into the prepared tin and smooth the top. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until the edges pull away from the tin and a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. The top should be a pale golden, not deeply browned. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes before cutting into squares. Serve warm.
